corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: no hover repair needed from the particle lane; detector must keep بُكْمٌ as one lexical host
what_failed: The particle detector can overreach if it treats an initial bā-like letter as an attached preposition without segmentation evidence.
sarf_lesson: A raw first letter is not a clitic. Require token-internal segmentation before routing to bā'/lām/fā'/kāf particle review.
nahw_lesson: Nahw should not receive a preposition/oath route unless sarf proves a real particle segment.
learner_explanation: The bā' in this word belongs to the word itself; it is not a separate 'with/by' piece.
drill_prompt: Explain why بُكْمٌ should not be split as بِـ + host, then name the route it should take instead.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: sequence particle and clause scope must be recorded before rich certification
what_failed: A readable standalone particle gloss was treated as enough even though the clause relation and scope were not attached.
sarf_lesson: No new sarf split is needed for a standalone ثُمَّ token; sarf should mark it as a function token and defer.
nahw_lesson: ثُمَّ must be linked to sequence/transition scope, not only translated as a dictionary particle.
learner_explanation: The word tells how one clause follows another; the hover should teach the connection, not just the English word 'then'.
drill_prompt: For ثُمَّ أَمَاتَهُ فَأَقْبَرَهُ, identify the previous/following actions and the sequence relation.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: yes/no question frame must be recorded before rich certification
what_failed: The hover exposed an English bundle instead of a single certified interrogative function.
sarf_lesson: A standalone هَلْ has no host split; sarf records the function-token surface and defers the question frame to nahw.
nahw_lesson: هَلْ introduces a yes/no question frame and must be linked to the following clause.
learner_explanation: هَلْ does not mean all English auxiliaries at once; it marks that the following clause is a question.
drill_prompt: Given هَلْ أَتَىٰكَ, identify the question particle and the following clause it governs.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: exception polarity, type, and case policy must be recorded before rich certification
what_failed: The hover string is plausible, but it does not teach the exceptive structure.
sarf_lesson: The particle is not a content noun or verb; sarf records the function-token surface and defers the exception frame.
nahw_lesson: إِلَّا requires mustathnā/minhu, polarity, exception type, and case behavior.
learner_explanation: To understand 'except', the learner needs to know what is being excepted and from what.
drill_prompt: For إِلَّا قَلِيلًا مِّنْهُمْ, identify the excepted noun and why its case matters.
